skill.name: ai-avatar-video
skill.description: Create AI avatar, talking-head, and lip-sync videos on RunComfy via the `runcomfy` CLI. Routes across ByteDance OmniHuman (audio-driven full-body avatar), Wan-AI Wan 2-7 (audio-driven mouth sync via `audio_url` on a portrait), HappyHorse 1.0 (Arena #1 t2v / i2v with in-pass audio), and Seedance v2 Pro (multi-modal cinematic with reference audio + reference subject).
skill.summary: Create AI avatar and talking-head videos by routing across RunComfy audio-driven models with the correct CLI commands.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 115 static findings from SKILL.md (303 lines, markdown only). All findings are false positives. The 67 external_commands flags detected Ruby/shell backtick patterns in markdown code block delimiters and inline code formatting, not actual shell execution. The 36 network URL findings are legitimate RunComfy service documentation links (runcomfy.com, docs.runcomfy.com, skills.sh). The 2 filesystem findings reference standard CLI token storage documentation (~/.config/runcomfy/token.json). The 5 weak-cryptography and 5 system-reconnaissance findings have no basis in this documentation-only file. The skill properly constrains shell usage via allowed-tools: Bash(runcomfy *) and includes a comprehensive security section with consent norms and input boundary documentation.

content.user_title: Create AI Avatar Videos and Talking Head Content
content.value_statement: Creating AI avatar and lip-sync videos requires navigating complex model pipelines with different input requirements. This skill automatically routes your request to the best RunComfy model - OmniHuman, Wan 2-7, HappyHorse, or Seedance - and generates the exact CLI invocation needed for each one.
actual_capabilities: Routes audio-driven avatar requests to the correct RunComfy model based on user intent
Generates complete runcomfy run CLI commands with the proper JSON input for each model
Supports OmniHuman, Wan 2-7, HappyHorse 1.0, Seedance v2 Pro, and Wan 2-2 Animate models
Handles both audio-file-driven generation and script-to-video workflow without an audio file
Provides model-specific prompting patterns and tips for optimal generation results
Chains with image generation skills for complete end-to-end avatar video production
limitations: Requires a RunComfy account and an active API token or CLI login to run commands
Cannot generate audio from text - needs a pre-recorded audio file or HappyHorse in-pass generation
Video quality depends on the quality of input images and audio files provided by the user
Limited to models available through the RunComfy CLI platform

use_cases.title: Content Creator Producing UGC Voiceover Videos
use_cases.description: A content creator needs multiple short-form videos with a consistent presenter avatar. The skill routes to OmniHuman with a portrait and voiceover audio file for each clip.

use_cases.title: Marketer Creating Multi-Language Product Demos
use_cases.description: A marketing team wants to dub product demo videos into different languages using the same presenter. The skill uses OmniHuman with one portrait and different audio files per language.

use_cases.title: Developer Building Script-to-Video Workflows
use_cases.description: A developer wants to generate talking-head videos from written scripts without audio files. The skill routes to HappyHorse 1.0 which generates speech from the script text.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Talking Head from Photo and Audio
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a talking head video of this person speaking. Use the portrait at [IMAGE_URL] and the voiceover at [AUDIO_URL] with OmniHuman.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a portrait photo and a pre-recorded voiceover audio file

prompt_templates.title: Video from Written Script Without Audio
prompt_templates.prompt: Make this portrait say: 'Welcome to our channel. Today we will show you how this works.' Use HappyHorse image-to-video with the portrait at [IMAGE_URL].
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a portrait image but no audio file

prompt_templates.title: Full Scene Lip-Sync with Custom Audio
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a video of a woman delivering a voiceover in a professional studio setting. Use Wan 2-7 with audio_url. Portrait at [IMAGE_URL], audio at [AUDIO_URL].
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs a complete generated scene with lip-sync, not just a portrait animation

prompt_templates.title: Cinematic Multi-Reference Avatar Production
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a cinematic monologue using Seedance v2 Pro. Reference image: [IMAGE_URL]. Reference audio: [AUDIO_URL]. Prompt: cinematic close-up, warm golden hour lighting, confident delivery to camera.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants cinematic quality with multiple reference inputs including image and audio

output_examples.input: Make this photo talk using the voiceover I recorded. Portrait is at https://example.com/presenter.jpg and audio is at https://example.com/voiceover.mp3.
output_examples.output: RunComfy OmniHuman processes your request. It generates a video file showing the portrait naturally speaking and gesturing to the audio track. The result is saved as an MP4 file in the output directory.

output_examples.input: Create a talking head video from this script: 'Hi everyone, welcome to our channel.' I have a photo but no audio file.
output_examples.output: The skill routes to HappyHorse 1.0 image-to-video. It generates speech from the script and creates a video where the portrait speaks the words. The output video is downloaded to the output directory.

best_practices: Use a clean head-and-shoulders portrait and high-quality audio for the best lip-sync results from OmniHuman
Match the emotional tone of the audio in the prompt to guide the model toward appropriate facial expressions
Keep HappyHorse scripts to 1-2 sentences per clip and chain multiple clips together for longer narratives
anti_patterns: Do not put spoken words in the prompt for audio-driven models - the audio drives the mouth, not the text prompt
Do not use Seedance v2 Pro for simple portrait-plus-audio tasks - OmniHuman is faster and produces better results
Do not use audio with background music for lip-sync - isolate the voice stem first for cleaner mouth sync

faq.question: What is the difference between OmniHuman and HappyHorse?
faq.answer: OmniHuman requires an audio file and creates a full-body avatar video. HappyHorse generates speech from a written script and does not need an external audio file.

faq.question: Do I need a RunComfy account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. You need to install the runcomfy CLI with npm and sign in with runcomfy login or set a RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable for CI environments.

faq.question: What image formats does the skill support?
faq.answer: The skill accepts any image URL that RunComfy models support. Common formats are JPEG and PNG. The image URL must be publicly accessible.

faq.question: Can I create a video without a reference image?
faq.answer: Yes. HappyHorse 1.0 text-to-video can generate a talking head from a text description alone without a reference image.

faq.question: How long can the generated videos be?
faq.answer: Duration depends on the model. HappyHorse works best with short clips of 1-2 sentences. Seedance v2 Pro allows up to 10 seconds for cinematic shots.

faq.question: Is this a free service?
faq.answer: The skill itself is free to use. RunComfy charges usage fees for model inference. Check runcomfy.com for current pricing details.
